title: eremophila incisa
intro: Eremophila incisa, also known as the Cut-Leaf Emu Bush, is a flowering plant native to Western Australia. It is a shrub with a dense growth habit, often used as a hedge or in landscaping due to its attractive white or purple flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its purported medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral effects. The Cut-Leaf Emu Bush can be prepared as a herbal remedy through infusions, tinctures, and ointments, which may help alleviate symptoms of colds and flu, as well as promoting wound healing and skin health.
